bearCases.bear_case: Enterprise agents stay in draft, copilot, or human-approval workflows and never receive broad production write permissions.
strengthens_if: Customer stories remain usage anecdotes without governed write-action proof.
Security and compliance language grows faster than deployment evidence.
weakens_if: Primary sources show agents executing production actions under policy controls.

bearCases.bear_case: TPU ownership reduces cost but does not protect Search economics or improve Cloud margin enough to matter.
strengthens_if: Capex rises while Cloud margin or AI revenue quality fails to improve.
Search monetization pressure accelerates.
weakens_if: Cloud margin and customer evidence support TPU-driven cost leverage.

bearCases.bear_case: AI ASIC wins are lumpy customer-concentration events rather than durable platform economics.
strengthens_if: AI revenue commentary depends on a small number of named hyperscaler programs.
Networking pull-through or VMware margin quality weakens.
weakens_if: Multiple hyperscaler programs and networking attach show repeatable demand.

bearCases.bear_case: Advanced packaging and leading-edge scarcity normalize while overseas fab costs pressure margins.
strengthens_if: Packaging bottleneck language fades and margin quality weakens.
Customer diversification shifts bargaining power away from TSMC.
weakens_if: HPC mix, pricing, and packaging commentary remain tight and margin-supportive.

bearCases.bear_case: NVIDIA becomes a high-quality accelerator vendor whose margins and attach rates compress as custom silicon scales.
strengthens_if: Gross margin compresses while custom silicon evidence rises.
Networking/software pull-through is not visible in disclosures.
weakens_if: Rack-scale architecture, networking, software, and margin evidence remain aligned.

bearCases.bear_case: Samsung or Micron catches up in HBM qualification and erodes SK Hynix leadership.
strengthens_if: Customer qualification evidence shifts away from SK Hynix.
HBM pricing or share commentary weakens.
weakens_if: Customer and company sources reinforce HBM leadership into next-generation platforms.

bearCases.bear_case: AI power demand exists, but grid, regulatory, and interconnection friction prevents earnings conversion.
strengthens_if: PJM or regulatory timelines slip without offsetting contracted economics.
PPA announcements do not become realized earnings evidence.
weakens_if: Primary regulatory or company evidence shows demand converting into contracted economics.

bearCases.bear_case: Power and cooling narratives stay strong while orders, backlog, or margins roll over.
strengthens_if: Orders/backlog slow before the AI capex story changes.
Liquid-cooling commentary remains promotional without financial pull-through.
weakens_if: Orders, backlog, margin, and cooling evidence remain mutually supportive.

bearCases.bear_case: AI becomes a feature inside existing products without changing workflow depth, retention, or revenue quality.
strengthens_if: Product language rises without retention or subscription evidence.
Customers treat AI as table stakes rather than a paid verifier layer.
weakens_if: AI workflow evidence links to subscription durability or product depth.

bearCases.bear_case: Credit-cycle noise overwhelms AI workflow benefits and AI does not improve analytics economics.
strengthens_if: Analytics growth or retention weakens while AI mentions remain vague.
Ratings-cycle pressure explains results better than product adoption.
weakens_if: AI tools show measurable workflow, retention, or analytics value.

bearCases.bear_case: LLM workflows bypass S&P Global's data products or compress data-licensing economics.
strengthens_if: Kensho/API/MCP references stay absent from customer or revenue evidence.
Data licensing weakens while AI usage grows elsewhere.
weakens_if: Trusted data products become embedded in AI workflows.

bearCases.bear_case: Regulated customers experiment with AI but do not adopt Veeva Agents in production workflows.
strengthens_if: AI Agents remain roadmap language or controlled demos.
CRM migration absorbs attention without AI workflow monetization.
weakens_if: Customer or product evidence shows regulated production workflow usage.

bearCases.bear_case: AI semitest demand normalizes before robotics optionality is large enough to matter.
strengthens_if: Semitest bookings weaken and robotics stays immaterial.
AI commentary does not convert into revenue durability.
weakens_if: Semitest demand remains strong or robotics begins to offset cyclicality.

bearCases.bear_case: Cash becomes a persistent drag if thesis evidence stays strong and valuation resets never arrive.
strengthens_if: Alerts stay green and cash drag dominates decision reviews.
Valuation bands do not create attractive review opportunities.
weakens_if: Drawdowns, valuation resets, or source-health issues make cash option value visible.

falsifierThresholds.threshold: No source-linked evidence that AI workflow adoption affects retention, product depth, or revenue by 2027-12-31.

falsifierThresholds.threshold: No source-linked evidence that Kensho, API, MCP, or data licensing embeds in AI workflows by 2027-12-31.

falsifierThresholds.decision_rule: If triggered, review whether AI Agents remain roadmap-only rather than a production workflow layer.

falsifierThresholds.threshold: Semiconductor-test demand weakens for 2 consecutive reviews before robotics revenue becomes material.

bear_cases.boundary: Bear-case movement changes review priority only; it is not a trade instruction.
bear_cases.purpose: Force each thesis to track a live opposing argument, not only confirming evidence.

falsifier_thresholds.boundary: Thresholds require a decision-log entry; they do not execute trades or rewrite weights automatically.
falsifier_thresholds.default_review_action: Open thesis review, update evidence log, and record whether conviction, thesis wording, or sizing policy changed.
falsifier_thresholds.purpose: Convert narrative falsifiers into operational human-review triggers.

valuation_bands.boundary: Bands trigger human valuation review only. They do not change target weights, conviction, or holdings.
valuation_bands.purpose: Make the valuation gate operational without creating automatic trading.

valuationBands.review_low: portfolio drawdown or valuation-reset risk rises

decisionLog.summary: Added a rules-first research monitor with source health, metric catalog, and LLM governance boundary.
Agent governance evidence improved, but production write-permission evidence remains insufficient.

evidenceLog.summary: No direct public evidence yet that Agent 365 is broadly executing governed production write actions.
Agent 365 improves the governance surface, but does not by itself prove broad production write-permission adoption.
Ironwood and TPU evidence supports the cost-control question, but Cloud margin proof remains a quarterly review item.
Rubin platform evidence supports rack-scale architecture, while attach-rate and margin durability remain unproven.
PJM interconnection timing remains a thesis risk; supplement current secondary reporting with primary PJM or company evidence.
Vertiv power and liquid-cooling evidence remains thesis-supportive, but orders and backlog still need quarterly confirmation.
Veeva AI Agents roadmap is relevant, but regulated production workflow usage is still not proven.
AI semiconductor demand remains thesis-relevant, while customer concentration and program lumpiness still require quarterly review.
AI demand and capacity-planning evidence supports the chokepoint thesis, but HPC mix and packaging scarcity require ongoing quarterly confirmation.
Moody's Research Assistant is a relevant workflow product, but adoption and analytics revenue impact remain unproven.
Kensho data-access and MCP evidence supports the agentic-data thesis, but monetization and workflow depth still need review.

policy.timeout: Source may be brittle or slow; do not treat as claim failure.

metricCatalog.core_question: Can Google turn TPU, DeepMind, Cloud, Search, and Workspace into a cost advantage?
falsifier: Search economics weaken while TPU differentiation fails to offset capex pressure.

evidence_state.latest_summary: Ironwood and TPU evidence supports the cost-control question, but Cloud margin proof remains a quarterly review item.

next_action.question: Check whether TPU and capex advantage appears in Cloud margin or AI revenue quality.
next_action.success_condition: Cloud margin, product disclosure, or customer evidence supports TPU-driven cost leverage.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: Cloud margin fails to show TPU or capex leverage.

metricCatalog.core_question: Can Broadcom keep scaling hyperscaler ASIC and networking exposure?
falsifier: Custom silicon wins do not translate into durable networking or platform pull-through.

evidence_state.latest_summary: AI semiconductor demand remains thesis-relevant, while customer concentration and program lumpiness still require quarterly review.

next_action.question: Check whether AI semiconductor growth is broadening beyond a small customer set.
next_action.success_condition: Management discloses durable ASIC or networking demand across multiple hyperscalers.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: AI semiconductor growth depends on too few customers.

metricCatalog.core_question: Does leading-edge foundry plus advanced packaging remain the AI silicon chokepoint?
falsifier: Advanced packaging no longer constrains AI silicon supply or margin quality degrades structurally.

evidence_state.latest_summary: AI demand and capacity-planning evidence supports the chokepoint thesis, but HPC mix and packaging scarcity require ongoing quarterly confirmation.

next_action.question: Review whether advanced packaging and leading-edge demand still support margin quality.
next_action.success_condition: HPC mix, margin, and packaging commentary remain thesis-supportive.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: Overseas fab cost or packaging constraints pressure margin quality.

metricCatalog.core_question: Is NVIDIA still the AI factory operating layer rather than only a GPU vendor?
falsifier: Rubin racks ship, but networking attach, software pull-through, and margins compress sharply.

evidence_state.latest_summary: Rubin platform evidence supports rack-scale architecture, while attach-rate and margin durability remain unproven.

next_action.question: Track networking attach, rack-scale platform evidence, and margin durability.
next_action.success_condition: Rubin or Blackwell platform evidence shows durable networking/software pull-through.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: Custom silicon bypass pressure rises without offsetting platform pull-through.

metricCatalog.core_question: Does SK Hynix maintain HBM leadership into the Vera Rubin generation?
falsifier: SK Hynix loses HBM leadership or NVIDIA qualification leverage into Rubin.

next_action.question: Track HBM qualification and shipment evidence into the Vera Rubin generation.
next_action.success_condition: Company or customer evidence supports continued HBM leadership and shipment leverage.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: Samsung or Micron narrows the HBM qualification and shipment gap.

metricCatalog.core_question: Does data-center power scarcity convert into earnings and contracted growth?
falsifier: Contracted data-center demand fails to overcome grid, regulatory, or interconnection constraints.

evidence_state.latest_summary: PJM interconnection timing remains a thesis risk; supplement current secondary reporting with primary PJM or company evidence.

next_action.question: Track PJM and interconnection conversion from data-center demand into realizable economics.
next_action.success_condition: Regulatory, PJM, or company evidence shows improved path from demand to contracted power economics.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: Interconnection delays prevent power scarcity from reaching realized economics.

metricCatalog.core_question: Does AI data-center power and cooling capex keep translating into orders and backlog?
falsifier: AI data-center capex fails to convert into durable Vertiv orders, backlog, or margins.

evidence_state.latest_summary: Vertiv power and liquid-cooling evidence remains thesis-supportive, but orders and backlog still need quarterly confirmation.

next_action.question: Check whether AI power and cooling capex is still visible in orders, backlog, and liquid-cooling commentary.
next_action.success_condition: Orders, backlog, and cooling commentary remain aligned with AI data-center demand.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: Orders or backlog slow while AI capex narratives remain strong.

metricCatalog.core_question: Can MSCI data and risk models become AI workflow verification infrastructure?
falsifier: Trusted data and risk workflows do not become outcome-verification revenue.

next_action.question: Look for evidence that MSCI risk and data products are becoming AI workflow verification assets.
next_action.success_condition: Product, retention, or revenue evidence links AI workflow adoption to trusted data demand.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: AI remains a product feature without measurable workflow adoption.

metricCatalog.core_question: Does AI strengthen Moody's credit-risk workflow rather than commoditize it?
falsifier: AI does not improve Moody's analytics or ratings workflow economics.

evidence_state.latest_summary: Moody's Research Assistant is a relevant workflow product, but adoption and analytics revenue impact remain unproven.

next_action.question: Check whether Moody's AI tools improve analytics workflow adoption or credit-risk research quality.
next_action.success_condition: Analytics growth, retention, or product evidence shows AI-enhanced workflow value.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: AI assistant evidence stays marketing-only while credit cycle weakens.

metricCatalog.core_question: Can Kensho and trusted data embed S&P Global inside LLM workflows?
falsifier: LLM workflow adoption bypasses S&P Global's data and ratings franchises.

evidence_state.latest_summary: Kensho data-access and MCP evidence supports the agentic-data thesis, but monetization and workflow depth still need review.

next_action.question: Look for Kensho API, MCP, or data-licensing evidence inside LLM workflows.
next_action.success_condition: Product or customer evidence shows trusted S&P data embedded in AI workflows.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: Kensho and trusted-data products do not show workflow integration evidence.

metricCatalog.core_question: Does regulated workflow AI become real production adoption in life sciences?
falsifier: Regulated customers do not adopt Veeva AI in production workflows.

evidence_state.latest_summary: Veeva AI Agents roadmap is relevant, but regulated production workflow usage is still not proven.

next_action.question: Track whether Veeva AI Agents move from roadmap to regulated production workflow usage.
next_action.success_condition: Official rollout, customer, or product evidence shows AI Agents used in regulated workflows.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: AI Agents remain roadmap-only or show low regulated-workflow usage.

metricCatalog.core_question: Is AI semiconductor test strong enough while robotics keeps option value?
falsifier: AI semiconductor-test demand normalizes before robotics optionality can matter.

metrics.name: Robotics revenue and factory automation deals

next_action.question: Check whether semiconductor test demand remains strong enough while robotics option value develops.
next_action.success_condition: Semitest bookings, AI revenue commentary, or robotics deal evidence supports the blended thesis.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: Semiconductor test demand digests before robotics becomes material.

metricCatalog.core_question: Does cash still have risk-control option value?
falsifier: Cash no longer provides useful option value against drawdown, valuation, or framework risk.

next_action.question: Review whether cash still has option value versus current alerts, drawdown, and valuation resets.
next_action.success_condition: Cash remains useful against drawdown, valuation, source-health, or framework uncertainty.

metricCatalog.watch_rule: Cash drag becomes the main portfolio error while thesis evidence remains strong.

principles.red_alert_policy: A red falsifier alert means the thesis must be rewritten or downgraded before any trade decision.

frameworkGaps.label: Agent cybersecurity control layer
frameworkGaps.reason: If trust is the framework bottleneck, identity, policy, endpoint, and network security should be tracked explicitly.
research_questions: Which public companies control policy enforcement for governed agent write actions?
Which security vendors show product evidence specific to agent permissions, identity, and runtime controls?

frameworkGaps.label: Third enterprise AI hyperscaler
frameworkGaps.reason: The framework owns MSFT and GOOGL, but a complete authority/capacity map should explain the absence of the third large hyperscaler.
research_questions: Does the cloud platform offset non-cloud business complexity enough to deserve framework inclusion?
Is the enterprise AI entry point differentiated enough versus current holdings?

frameworkGaps.label: Edge and consumer trust distribution
frameworkGaps.reason: Device-side and consumer distribution may represent a different trusted-execution path than enterprise cloud.
research_questions: Does on-device AI create a durable trust and privacy control layer?
Does consumer-scale distribution create a control right that the current framework underweights?

frameworkGaps.label: Non-AI-capex defensive ballast
frameworkGaps.reason: Cash is explicit, but the framework should track whether a non-capex operating asset would improve decision quality.
research_questions: Which assets are genuinely uncorrelated to hyperscaler AI capex rather than merely in another thesis bucket?
What evidence would justify adding a ballast bucket without diluting the trusted-execution thesis?

hyperscaler_capex_cycle.rationale: HBM leadership depends on AI accelerator demand and customer qualification cycles.
AI ASIC and networking growth is concentrated in hyperscaler programs.
Cash is the explicit risk-control reserve against synchronized thesis and valuation resets.
Data-center power scarcity only monetizes if AI load growth converts into contracted demand.
TPU, Cloud, Search AI, and Workspace monetization are tied to AI capex and usage quality.
Credit-risk workflow demand is less directly tied to hyperscaler capex.
Outcome-verification workflow adoption may lag AI capex and is less directly tied to buildout cycles.
Cloud and agent monetization still depend on enterprise AI infrastructure spending and adoption.
Data-center GPU, networking, and rack-scale demand are exposed to AI factory capex.
Data and ratings workflow adoption is a lagged trusted-data thesis, not a direct capex supplier thesis.
Semiconductor-test demand is levered to AI device complexity and capex timing.
Leading-edge and advanced-packaging demand are tied to AI accelerator buildouts.
Regulated workflow AI adoption is driven by life-sciences software cycles more than AI factory capex.
Power and cooling orders depend on AI data-center capex durability.

policy.boundary: Risk overlays create research reviews only; they do not change holdings or target weights.
policy.purpose: Track portfolio-level framework risks that are hidden by thesis buckets.

riskFactors.label: Hyperscaler AI capex cycle
riskFactors.review_policy: If direct target exposure stays above threshold, keep a portfolio-level framework-risk review open.
riskFactors.thesis_test: A synchronized hyperscaler capex slowdown should be treated as one portfolio-level risk, not nine separate holding stories.

sourceHealth.label: Yahoo Finance via yfinance
sourceHealth.notes: Unofficial research-only price source. Avoid treating it as licensed real-time market data.

sourceHealth.label: Local portfolio calculation
sourceHealth.notes: Calculated from versioned lots, target weights, prices, and FX.

sourceHealth.label: SEC EDGAR APIs and RSS
sourceHealth.notes: Free official source for US issuer filings and XBRL facts.

sourceHealth.label: Source link-health snapshots
sourceHealth.notes: Checks link availability and source brittleness without treating bot-blocked pages as false claims.

sourceHealth.label: Company IR and press releases
sourceHealth.notes: Cleanest source for earnings releases, product launches, and management commentary.

sourceHealth.label: FRED macro data
sourceHealth.notes: Good fit for rates, inflation, industrial production, and credit proxies.

sourceHealth.label: News RSS and GDELT-style monitoring
sourceHealth.notes: Useful for event discovery, not for uncited thesis changes.

thesisChangelog.new_summary: Microsoft as authority-control asset, with production write-permission adoption still unproven.
thesisChangelog.reason: Avoid overstating product existence as production adoption.

thesisChangelog.new_summary: Rubin platform evidence supports architecture, while attach rate and margin durability remain separate evidence questions.
thesisChangelog.previous_summary: Rubin platform evidence supports infrastructure moat.
thesisChangelog.reason: Architecture strength is not automatically monetization durability.

thesisChangelog.new_summary: PJM and interconnection claims should be supplemented with primary PJM, regulatory, or company sources.
thesisChangelog.previous_summary: Three Mile Island and PJM timing risk tracked through secondary reporting.
thesisChangelog.reason: Nodal power thesis needs primary-source provenance before confidence can increase.
